# Questions in Livestream 51

Live Stream #51: Manna Machines Over Area 51.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=429" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:09</a> | Does Exodus 21:22-25 teach that the Bible does not affirm the personhood of a fetus, and does the Bible touch on the issue? | He says Exodus 21 addresses accidental injury case law rather than fetal personhood, but other biblical passages do touch on personhood before birth. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=887" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:47</a> | How did Egyptians keep track of years before AD/BC? | He says they counted by regnal years rather than by a single era system. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=1110" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:30</a> | How can God's command to destroy the Amalekites in 1 Samuel 15:3, including women and infants, be morally justified? | He frames the issue through ancient clan justice and warns against judging the ancient context by modern assumptions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=1573" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:13</a> | Which Hebrew should someone study for biblical exegesis? | He says most students should begin with Biblical Hebrew rather than Modern Hebrew if exegesis is the goal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=1721" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:41</a> | What books help with biblical poetic language, symbolism, prophecy, and ancient linguistics? | He recommends Robert Alter's work on biblical poetry for poetics, while leaving ancient linguistics for more advanced language study. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=1864" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:04</a> | How should the thousand years in Revelation 20 be understood, and could the timing differ between Satan being bound and the saints ruling with Christ? | He says the thousand years may function as a long symbolic period, and the experience of time could differ even if chronological time is similar. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=2186" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:26</a> | Is there evidence that the New Testament was originally written in Hebrew, or that the Septuagint was the original manuscript? | He says no; the Septuagint is a Greek Old Testament translation, and the New Testament does not show evidence of being translated from Hebrew. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=2509" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:49</a> | Could Tall el-Hammam be Sodom? | He says no and outlines major chronological, geographic, publication, stratigraphic, and professional-practice problems with that identification. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=3002" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:02</a> | If preterism were true, could Daniel 7 describe Christianity taking over Rome, ending paganism, and spreading worldwide? | He says that reading still does not persuade him and would depend on assuming preterism first. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=3443" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:23</a> | Does Numbers 5:23-28 provide biblical grounds for abortion? | He says no; he reads the passage as a jealousy ritual and judgment on an unfaithful spouse, not as an abortive procedure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=3762" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:42</a> | Could Moses have taken a hallucinogenic drug when he saw the burning bush? | He says archaeopharmacology is a real field and some ancient plants may have had effects, but he doubts Moses was high at the burning bush. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=3933" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:33</a> | Does Genesis 1 imply angelic beings are made in the image of God or had a hand in creation? | He answers no to both, saying the image of God is specific to humanity and the creation language is better read through God, not angelic co-creators. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=4229" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:29</a> | What is the best way to translate Elohim in the serpent's statement to Eve: "like God" or "like the gods"? | He says "like God" is the more probable reading and notes that the plural "you" implies Adam was present too. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=4510" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:10</a> | In Matthew 16:18, is the rock Kepha himself or the truth that Jesus is the Messiah? | He argues the rock is Christ as the cornerstone, not Peter himself, while acknowledging that Peter is still a rock in another sense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=4840" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:40</a> | What is the context of Eve's statement about Seth in Genesis 4:25, and is Seth a descendant of Adam? | He says Seth functions as Abel's clan replacement, and the text may be compressed enough that direct son versus later descendant is not the main point. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=5207" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:47</a> | If you started a ska band, would you call it "Oh Boy"? | He says he would not start a ska band because he does not like ska. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=5280" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:00</a> | What is your opinion of Bart Ehrman's scholarship: good faith, strong scholarship, or weak arguments for rhetorical purposes? | He says Ehrman does both: his text-critical scholarship can be good, but many popular arguments reflect a weak fundamentalist-versus-contradiction framework. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=6090" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:30</a> | When Pharaoh Necho II says God told him to fight in 2 Chronicles 35:20-21, did God really speak to him or is the text confused? | He says the text is not necessarily confused; Necho may have believed a god spoke to him, but the passage leaves the details ambiguous. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=6367" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:07</a> | Were Cain, Abel, and Seth direct sons of Adam and Eve, or later clan progenitors from Adam and Eve's genetic line? | He thinks Cain and Abel were direct or early descendants, while Seth was likely a later progenitor. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=6456" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:36</a> | In Genesis 41:41-42, how common was it for viziers in Egypt to receive Joseph's clothing and honors? | He says the fine linen and signet fit vizier office, while the gold necklace was a special honor not given to every vizier. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=6808" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:28</a> | How much evidence of boxing or martial arts appears throughout ancient history? | He says evidence for boxing is limited, but Egypt has strong evidence for systematic martial arts, especially in Middle Kingdom wrestling scenes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=7036" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:16</a> | Since Yahweh may have come from Edom, what about the view that Yahweh was an Edomite god like Qos? | He says it is possible but uncertain because records from Edom are scarce, and the evidence is not enough to be confident. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=7245" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:45</a> | How many pharaohs do we know of who ordered the genocidal murder of babies from an ethnic group? | He says none outside Exodus; if the event is historical, likely candidates would be limited to a small set of late 18th or 19th Dynasty rulers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=7467" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:27</a> | How can someone get a literature PhD and funding with a 3.2 undergraduate GPA in communications at age 28? | He advises using a master's degree to prove graduate-level strength, rehabilitate the GPA, and test whether the PhD path fits. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=7898" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:38</a> | Could there be other advanced agricultural civilizations similar to Egypt but older, perhaps 20,000 years old? | He says older finds are possible, but current evidence points to early agricultural civilizations around eight to twelve thousand years ago. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=8127" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:27</a> | How did waste management work in the Bronze Age? | He says garbage pits were common and are a major source of archaeological data. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=8342" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:02</a> | Are the incest prohibitions in Leviticus 18 partly tied to Ramesses II's practice? | He says no; he sees Leviticus as concerned with normal versus abnormal categories, not as a response to Ramesses II. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=8730" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:30</a> | Could the Tower of Babel have been at Eridu? | He discusses the theory but does not accept it, saying the Eridu-expansion evidence is too thin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=8883" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:03</a> | Are you familiar with Jungian types? | He says psychology is not his field and he cannot answer the question well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=8951" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:11</a> | What is your take on Mark 13:19 and the eschaton? | He reads the passage with a futurist leaning and says the predicted tribulation seems worse than past events such as the siege of Jerusalem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=9505" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:38:25</a> | What are your thoughts on Asherah and Yahweh being worshiped together, and on the Kuntillet Ajrud inscription? | He says the inscription likely refers to "his asherah" as an object, not Yahweh's wife, though he allows that Yahweh and an asherah object may have been associated in worship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=9888" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:48</a> | What do you think about the upcoming public UFO hearings? | He offers speculative but cautious thoughts, saying UFOs appear to be real phenomena but that does not prove an extraordinary explanation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=10135" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:55</a> | What are your thoughts on Cassuto's work on the documentary hypothesis? | He says Cassuto's work is strong and thorough but dated, because the documentary hypothesis has changed and later work has superseded parts of it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=10374" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:54</a> | If you had to guess, where was the Garden of Eden located? | He says the answer depends heavily on one's flood model, with northern Mesopotamia fitting best under a local-flood reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=10517" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:17</a> | Is Leviticus 17:11 contradicted by Leviticus 5:11, since flour is not blood? | He says no; Leviticus 17 gives a general principle, while Leviticus 5 concerns a broader guilt or purification offering with concessions for poverty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/w-YYxBd2iRw?t=11321" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:41</a> | What clues show parts of the Torah are older than J, P, E, and D? | He points to Egyptian loanwords, geographic details, and other data that he thinks preserve Late Bronze Age core material. |
